title: Zornia tetraphylla
taxonomy: Zornia tetraphylla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. This plant belongs to the genus Zornia, which is a group of annual or perennial herbs native to the Americas. Zornia tetraphylla is further classified as part of the subfamily Faboideae, which includes a wide range of legume species. Its classification also places it in the tribe Dalbergieae, a group of flowering plants known for their unique characteristics. The exact classification of Zornia tetraphylla is Zornia tetraphylla (L.) Pers. ex Willd.
uses: Zornia tetraphylla is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. intro: Zornia tetraphylla, also known as "four-leafed zornia" or "pata de perro," is a plant species in the legume family (Fabaceae) native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Zornia tetraphylla include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of tinctures, teas, and infusions to leverage its health benefits.
